title: Geology & Earth Science
description: Rocks, minerals, geologic time, and Earth processes

cards.front: What are the three main types of rock?
cards.back: Igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic
Rock formed from cooled and solidified magma or lava

cards.front: Rock formed from cooled magma or lava
cards.back: Igneous rock
Rock formed from compressed and cemented layers of sediment

cards.front: Rock formed from layers of compressed sediment
cards.back: Sedimentary rock
Rock that has been changed by heat and pressure from a pre-existing rock

cards.front: Rock changed by heat and pressure
cards.back: Metamorphic rock
A coarse-grained intrusive igneous rock (cooled slowly underground)
A fine-grained extrusive igneous rock — the most common rock on Earth's surface
Volcanic glass formed when lava cools very rapidly

cards.front: Volcanic glass formed from rapidly cooled lava
cards.back: Obsidian
A light, porous igneous rock that can float on water

cards.front: Igneous rock light enough to float on water
cards.back: Pumice
A sedimentary rock made mainly of calcium carbonate, often from marine organisms
A sedimentary rock formed from cemented sand-sized grains
A metamorphic rock formed from limestone subjected to heat and pressure
Marble
A metamorphic rock formed from shale subjected to heat and pressure
Slate
A hard metamorphic rock formed from sandstone subjected to heat and pressure
Quartzite
Talc
Gypsum
Calcite
Fluorite
Apatite
Orthoclase (feldspar)
Quartz
Topaz
Corundum
Diamond

cards.front: The four eons of geologic time (oldest to newest)
cards.back: Hadean, Archean, Proterozoic, Phanerozoic

cards.front: The three eras of the Phanerozoic Eon (oldest to newest)
cards.back: Paleozoic, Mesozoic, Cenozoic
A rapid diversification of animal life about 541 million years ago, early in the Paleozoic Era

cards.front: Rapid diversification of animal life ~541 million years ago
cards.back: Cambrian Explosion
A period of the Mesozoic Era (~201–145 million years ago) known for the dominance of dinosaurs
Mass extinction ~66 million years ago that wiped out non-avian dinosaurs, likely caused by an asteroid impact

cards.front: Mass extinction ~66 million years ago that killed the dinosaurs
cards.back: Cretaceous–Paleogene (K–Pg) extinction event
The supercontinent that existed about 335–175 million years ago before breaking apart

cards.front: Supercontinent that included all major landmasses ~250 million years ago
cards.back: Pangaea
A plate boundary where two tectonic plates move toward each other
A plate boundary where two tectonic plates move apart from each other
A plate boundary where two tectonic plates slide horizontally past each other
The process where one tectonic plate slides beneath another at a convergent boundary

cards.front: One tectonic plate sliding beneath another
cards.back: Subduction
An underwater mountain range formed by divergent tectonic plates on the ocean floor
A horseshoe-shaped zone of frequent earthquakes and volcanic eruptions around the Pacific Ocean

cards.front: Zone of volcanoes and earthquakes ringing the Pacific Ocean
cards.back: Ring of Fire
The second most abundant mineral in Earth's crust; hardness 7 on Mohs scale; made of silicon dioxide
The most abundant mineral group in Earth's crust; a key component of granite
A mineral group known for splitting into thin, flexible, shiny sheets

cards.front: Mineral that splits into thin, shiny, flexible sheets
cards.back: Mica
The main mineral in limestone and marble; hardness 3; fizzes with acid
The mineral name for rock salt (sodium chloride)
Halite
An iron sulfide mineral known as 'fool's gold' for its metallic yellow appearance
Pyrite

cards.front: Earth's four main layers (surface to center)
cards.back: Crust, mantle, outer core, inner core
Earth's thin, solid outermost layer (5–70 km thick)
Earth's thickest layer, made of hot semi-solid rock between the crust and outer core
Mantle
The liquid layer of iron and nickel that surrounds Earth's inner core and generates the magnetic field
The solid, extremely hot center of the Earth, made mostly of iron and nickel
The preserved remains or traces of ancient organisms found in rock

cards.front: Preserved remains or traces of ancient organisms in rock
cards.back: Fossil
The breakdown of rocks and minerals at Earth's surface by water, wind, ice, or living organisms

cards.front: Breaking down of rocks at Earth's surface
cards.back: Weathering
The transport of weathered rock and soil by water, wind, ice, or gravity

cards.front: Transport of rock and soil by water, wind, or ice
cards.back: Erosion
A logarithmic scale used to measure the magnitude of earthquakes

cards.front: Logarithmic scale for measuring earthquake magnitude
cards.back: Richter scale

cards.front: What is magma called after it reaches Earth's surface?
cards.back: Lava
The largest mass extinction in Earth's history (~252 million years ago), killing ~96% of marine species — called 'The Great Dying'

cards.front: Largest mass extinction in Earth's history, called 'The Great Dying'
cards.back: Permian–Triassic extinction event (~252 million years ago)
